evidence.title: Al Jazeera report: June 14, 2025 Iran cancels next US nuclear talks amid Israeli attacks
evidence.source_description: Al Jazeera English article; extracted relevant page text from web retrieval.
evidence.relevance: Contemporaneous reporting on the status of U.S.-Iran talks immediately before the June 15, 2025 cutoff, including quoted Iranian foreign-ministry position and the scheduled sixth round in Oman.

evidence.title: White House fact sheet: February 6, 2026 statement that Iran refused to reach a deal in June
evidence.source_description: White House fact sheet; extracted page text from web retrieval.
evidence.relevance: Later official U.S. government characterization of June 2025 events, directly bearing on whether the U.S. position was that Iran had refused to reach a deal rather than agreed to permanent peace by the cutoff.

evidence.title: Official Iranian President website: June 14, 2025 Pezeshkian statement on Iran-US talks and US insincerity
evidence.source_description: Official website of the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran; extracted page text from web retrieval.
evidence.relevance: Official Iranian government statement on June 14, 2025, one day before the cutoff, addressing Iran-US talks, US trustworthiness, and ongoing hostilities; material to whether Iran publicly confirmed a permanent peace deal by the deadline.

evidence.title: White House release: June 17, 2025 Trump statements that Iran cannot have nuclear weapon
evidence.source_description: White House release; extracted page text from web retrieval.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. government release two days after the cutoff listing President Trump's Iran statements, including June 14-17 entries, material to whether the United States had confirmed a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2025.
